segments.title: Veo 2025 Performance Review -- Ridership Growth, Investment, and Equity Programs
segments.summary: Chair Nave opens Public Transportation Committee to discuss Veo contract renewal. Veo representatives Jeffrey Hoover, Paul Kalabufo, and Reed Neil present 2025 results: $250K total new investment including $50K city revenue share (15c/trip), $130K in opportunity zone rider rebates, expanded operations staffing, and community grants to Creek Rats. Key growth metrics: 34% ridership increase, 1.7 rides/vehicle/day (nearly double 'good' benchmark), 30K+ unique riders (20% increase), median trip cost down 2%. Veo Access (low-income) memberships up 90%. Tipover detection launched March 2025 with proactive cure time of 4.5 hours (70% improvement). Virtual parking coach prevented 10K bad parking instances since August. Data shows majority of riders are NOT Syracuse University students -- ridership peaks in summer when university is out of session. Significant proportion lack driver's licenses and live in low-income households. Syracuse outperforms peer cities (Rochester, Toledo, Hartford, Cleveland) on Access program.

'VO' throughout transcript = Veo (auto-transcription artifact)
Creek Rats = local Syracuse volunteer/community group receiving Veo community grants

segments.title: Revenue Share, Automated Tipover Detection, Opportunity Zones, and Virtual Parking Coach
segments.summary: Detailed breakdown of Veo's 2025 operational improvements. Revenue share: 15c/trip yielded ~$50K to city (exempting Access and opportunity zone trips). Automated tipover detection reduced proactive cure time from 16 hours to 4 hours 45 minutes despite 140 more tipped vehicles per day -- unprecedented improvement across all Veo markets. Opportunity zones designed with DPW based on low car ownership, household income, and transit gaps: riders automatically refunded $1 unlock fee when ending in designated areas, $130K+ returned to riders, zones expanded mid-year and flexible to council input. Virtual parking coach (AI-powered, August launch): 10K corrective parking actions in real time. New real-time rider analysis rolling out: detects hard swerves, impacts, and auto-slows speed; vehicle 'perception' system takes damaged vehicles out of service. 311-style complaint response: 92% under 3 hours (up from 77%), median 2hr 11min. Van fleet up 33%, labor hours up 22%, 40+ Syracuse residents employed, 80%+ city proper residents.
processing_notes: IPS in detected_entities is false positive from 'tips' substring in 'tipover'; excluded
$50K and $130K amounts are same data points from segment 1 presented in greater detail
DPW referenced as partner in designing opportunity zones

segments.title: Inclusive Vehicle Designs, Centro Transit Integration, and Intoxication/Helmet Safety Tools
segments.summary: Veo presents two new vehicle types: three-wheeled trike for riders with mobility limitations (storage capacity for oxygen tanks, walkers, larger bags) and cargo bike targeting food desert areas (zip code 13205 identified). Centro transit integration announced: riders connecting to any Centro bus stop via QR code scan receive $1 rebate -- no cost to Centro or city. Discussion of 44 existing Veo bike racks citywide (25 pre-existing, rest converted from prior bike share program), with council requesting more at bus stops. Nave raises intoxication concerns; Veo developing cognition test tool for spring 2026 to increase friction for impaired riders at targeted times. AI-powered helmet detection tool offers rebates as incentive (carrot approach) since NY state law does not require helmets for riders 18+. Equity concerns raised: helmet mandates and ID requirements could become access barriers. Council presses on minors accessing service despite 18+ terms of service -- transitions to extended discussion in next segment.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on minor rider safety, intoxication risks, and helmet enforcement gaps

segments.title: Battery Management, Mid-Ride Motor Cutoff Safety, and Vehicle Damage Liability
segments.summary: Council questions on practical operations: battery range (45 miles full charge, swap every 10 rides, removed from app at 5%), low-funds notification (verbal alert from bike, $20 pre-authorization to prevent mid-ride cutoff). Council member reports witnessing near-accident when bike motor cut off mid-ride near a bus -- Veo clarifies motor disengages but does not apply brakes. Damage liability: rider bears responsibility; Veo tracks all ride paths via 4-second GPS pings but legally requires subpoena for personally identifiable data (even for SPD). Council member notes bikes left on lawns remain biggest constituent complaint -- Council President Paniagua had one for 4 days. Virtual parking coach penalty: meter runs up to 20 minutes if rider ignores parking instructions. Charging racks technically possible but require grid tie-in; swappable batteries preferred for operational flexibility. DPW partnership sought for additional bike rack deployment.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on mid-ride safety incidents, damage liability processes, and persistent parking/lawn complaints from constituents

$6 and $20 amounts relate to pre-authorization discussion, not contract costs

segments.title: Minor Rider Access, Age Verification Equity, and Multilingual Community Outreach
segments.summary: Extended discussion on minor riders: California requires ID scan by state law but New York does not; parents can enable children using their own accounts. Veo argues mandatory ID verification creates equity barriers for unbanked/undocumented riders and could push minors to unsafe unregulated e-bikes. Council proposes teen rider program (14-17) with speed-limited bikes; Veo says technically possible but requires NY state law change plus new insurance product -- 2-year horizon, parallel effort underway with City of Denver. Access program challenges: income verification friction and language barriers despite app auto-translation to phone language (Arabic specifically confirmed). Paul Kalabufo describes community outreach: partnered with Nicole Watt from Hope Speak Open for 9-language event on north side teaching New Americans to use scooters -- north side subsequently showed highest Access program usage. Veo commits to establishing equity working group with city partnership to reach riders through public housing, libraries, and community organizations.

political_signal.description: Council pressing on child safety gaps in age verification while Veo highlights equity trade-offs of mandatory ID requirements

segments.title: Fleet Maintenance Diagnostics, Lithium Battery Fire Safety, and Rider Violation Enforcement
segments.summary: Paul Kalabufo details four-tier maintenance system: self-diagnostic error mode (removes vehicle from app), customer phone reports, systematic weekly rotation (10 scooters/week through full fleet), and seasonal full-fleet overhaul. All vehicles include self-sealing tires and brake failure detection. Five-step safety field check ('5 S's') performed on every vehicle touch -- given battery swap and tipover retrieval frequency, checks happen multiple times per week on most vehicles. Adding baskets to all scooters for grocery shopping. Lithium battery safety: UL-certified at cell, battery, and vehicle-electrical-system levels; practices aligned with FDNY as national leader on battery fire issues. Damaged battery protocol: visual inspection on every warehouse return, fireproof barrel storage with 'cell block' powder that vitrifies to smother fire, recycling through Redwood Materials. Warehouse electrical work done to city code with local official inspections. Violations up 51% year-over-year (outpacing 34% ride increase): two warnings then monetary fines then suspension requiring rules quiz -- approximately 200 riders suspended in 2025. Double riding prohibited; new two-person tandem vehicle coming. Meeting adjourns.
processing_notes: FDNY referenced as national leader in lithium battery safety practices -- Veo aligns Syracuse operations with FDNY standards
Cell block = fire suppression material that vitrifies (turns to glass bubble) when heated to smother battery fires
Paniagua left meeting early; her question about repeat rule-breakers relayed by Nave
Head mechanic for Veo's entire northeast region is based in Syracuse -- grew from driver to regional lead
Redwood Materials = lithium battery recycling partner; AutoZone also mentioned as recycling option
